data.title: Phoenix Group promotes Group Managing Director and co-founder, Munaf Ali as CEO
data.description:
• Phoenix Group (OTCPK:PNXGF) has appointed Munaf Ali as CEO. Ali, currently serves as Group Managing Director and co-founder of the company.
  • ALi succeeds Seyed Mohammad Alizadehfard, the current CEO and co-founder of Phoenix Group, will step down to manage Cypher Capital, but will remain a major shareholder and trusted advisor.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: FDX, X and more
data.description:
Stock futures slipped during Friday's premarket session as investors eagerly awaited the release of the Federal Reserve's most preferred inflation measure, November's personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index report, scheduled for release later today.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • FedEx (NYSE:FDX) shares surged nearly 9% despite mixed FQ2 results, as the parcel giant announced plans for a tax-free separation of its FedEx Freight unit into an independent public entity to streamline operations. While lowering its annual forecast, FedEx now expects flat revenue for the year, compared to the previously projected low single-digit growth, and adjusted EPS guidance was revised to $19.00-$20.00 ($19.50 midpoint), slightly below the prior range of $20.00-$21.00. The company cited weaker-than-expected revenue and profit at FedEx Freight, driven by reduced U.S. industrial production, which impacted demand in the less-than-truckload (LTL) industry. However, cost reduction efforts under the DRIVE program and higher base yields helped offset some of the freight unit's weakness. CEO Raj Subramaniam said the separation aims to unlock value for the freight business and position FedEx for greater shareholder returns.
  • Mission Produce (NASDAQ:AVO) shares jumped over 11% after FQ4 results exceeded expectations, driven by a 37.4% rise in sales and a 160% increase in adjusted profits, fueled by higher avocado prices. For FQ1, the company expects stable industry avocado volumes but anticipates a 20% rise in pricing, signaling continued strong demand.

Biggest stock losers

  • United States Steel (NYSE:X) shares dropped over 4% after the company lowered its Q4 guidance, citing weak demand and pricing in Europe. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter is now expected to be around $150M, down from the prior forecast of $225M to $275M, while adjusted net earnings per diluted share are projected between -$0.29 and -$0.25, much below the consensus of $0.28. CEO David Burritt highlighted continued pressure from low steel prices and ramp-related costs at Big River Steel, alongside reduced selling prices, volumes, and increased maintenance in the Flat-Rolled segment. The company also expects weaker adjusted EBITDA in its Mini Mill and European segments due to lower volumes.
  • Nike (NYSE:NKE) shares fell as much as 4% despite exceeding estimates in fiscal Q2, as the sportswear giant outlined its turnaround plan under new CEO Elliott Hill. The company is taking bold steps to revive the brand, including aggressively clearing aged inventory, strengthening its digital presence, and curbing promotional pricing. However, these initiatives will take time and require significant investment, with Nike warning of headwinds to its financial performance for the remainder of the year. The company projects Q3 revenue to decline by a low double-digit percentage, falling short of analyst expectations for an 8% drop, alongside a 300-350 basis point contraction in gross margin. Additionally, Nike expects an even steeper revenue decline in Q4 and anticipates its 2025 summer order books will be lower than last year. “This will take time,” Hill said during his first earnings call as CEO, “But I’m confident we are making the right move.”

data.title: Toyoda Gosei acquires stake in EV Motors Japan
data.description:
• Toyoda Gosei (OTCPK:TGOSY) has acquired a stake in EV Motors Japan Co., Ltd. (EVM-J), a startup involved in the development and marketing of commercial electric vehicles, including trucks and buses.
  • With the development, Toyoda Gosei (OTCPK:TGOSY) aims to learn on-site EV development and manufacturing processes through personnel exchanges and collaborative activities, expanding its knowledge of new mobility.
  • The acquisition was done through its Corporate Venture Capital Dept., an internal organization dedicated to these investments.
  • The commercial EVs developed by EVM-J is developing commercial EVs with a high-efficiency motor control system, achieving lower power loss, longer driving range, and reduced battery load. As EVM-J develops a production network in Japan, the company is building Japan's first mass production assembly plant in Kitakyushu City, integrating Zero Emission e-PARK.

data.title: STEP Energy Services terminates arrangement due to lack of shareholder approval
data.description:
• STEP Energy Services (OTCPK:SNVVF) announced that starting December 19, STEP, 2659160 Alberta Ltd., and the limited partnerships involved in ARC Energy Fund 8 (a private equity fund managed by ARC Financial Corp.) have executed a termination agreement that mutually ends the arrangement agreement initially established on November 3, 2024.
  • The decision to terminate was made after it became clear that the requisite minority shareholder approval could not be achieved.

  • "This outcome does not change our strategy for 2025 and onward. Our recently announced 2025 capital budget will continue to put STEP at the forefront of the evolving technology in this industry. We see some near term margin pressure, but we have a constructive outlook for 2025, particularly as we see additional LNG capacity coming on stream in the second half of the year," said Steve Glanville, President and CEO.

data.title: European markets lower after Trump spooks investors with tariffs
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.38% Retail sales in the U.K. rises less than expected.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.93%

France (CAC:IND) -0.96% Producer prices in the French domestic market climbed by 3.2% M/M in November.

The manufacturing confidence indicator in Denmark rose in December.

The Economic Tendency Indicator for Sweden held steady at 97.5 in December; The consumer confidence indicator in Sweden dropped to 96.7 in December. Producer prices in Sweden rose by 0.3% Y/Y in November.

Denmark’s economy expanded by 0.9% on quarter in Q3.

Switzerland's current account surplus narrowed in Q3.

Industrial production in Moldova fell by 8.5% Y/Y in October.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.89% lower, extending losses from previous day and monitoring political turmoil in the U.S. and monetary policy decisions from across the globe. Trump's comments about potential tariffs on the EU spooked investors in an already worried cautious market.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on the economic calendar front.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield down 1 basis point to 4.56%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up less than 1 basis point to 2.31%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 3 basis points to 4.61%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: Trump transfers entire DJT stake to trust, with son as sole trustee
data.description:
• President-elect Donald Trump has transferred his entire stake in Trump Media & Technology (NASDAQ:DJT) as a "bona fide gift" to a revocable trust, of which he is the sole beneficiary.
  • He transferred 114.75M Trump Media (NASDAQ:DJT) shares to the Donald J. Trump Revocable Trust on Tuesday for no consideration, according to a regulatory filing.
  • Following the transaction, Trump now indirectly owns DJT shares that were transferred to the trust.
  • His son, Donald Trump Jr., is the sole trustee and has sole voting and investment power over all securities owned by the trust.
  • The stake, which represented around 53% of Trump Media's (DJT) outstanding shares, is valued at $4.06B as of the stock's last closing price of $35.41/share.
  • Trump last month said he had no intention of selling his stake in the company.
  • Shares of Trump Media (DJT), which operates the Truth Social app, have more than doubled in value this year and rose 17.6% over the past month.

data.title: Africa Energy enters deal with Arostyle to restructure joint investment in Main Street 1549 Proprietary
data.description:
• Africa Energy (OTCPK:HPMCF) has entered into a non-binding agreement with Arostyle Investments Proprietary Ltd. to restructure their joint investment in Main Street 1549 Proprietary Ltd.

  • The firms, via Main Street 1549 Proprietary, presently hold a direct 10% participating stake in Block 11B/12B off the coast of South Africa.

  • The company currently owns 49% of the common shares and 100% of Class B shares of Main Street, while Arostyle holds the remaining 51% of the common shares.

  • Following the joint venture partners' withdrawal announced on July 29, and pending regulatory approvals, Main Street plans to take full ownership of the Block 11B/12B project.

  • Under the latest pact, Main Street is anticipated to be restricted as Africa Energy will hold a 75% direct interest in Block 11B/12B, while Arostyle will hold 25%.

  • Both parties aim to finalize the restructuring details by January 31.

data.title: Aadi Bioscience rockets 72% on key updates
data.description:
• Aadi Bioscience (NASDAQ:AADI) stock price soared 72% on Thursday during after market hours of trading as it signed an exclusive license agreement for Three-Asset ADC portfolio developed through a collaboration between WuXi Biologics (OTCPK:WXXWY) and HANGZHOU DAC.

  • The company will also sell FYARRO and associated infrastructure to KAKEN Pharmaceutical (OTCPK:KKPCF) for $100 million and also get PIPE financing of $100 million.

  • The capital will be used to fund operations into late 2028, including anticipated clinical data for the ADC portfolio.

  • Co-Founder and Former CEO of ProfoundBio, Baiteng Zhao, is appointed to Aadi (AADI) Board of Directors.

More on Aadi Bioscience
